basic_details.job_title: RITES Individual Consultants Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 35 Section Engineer, Geologist and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Individual Consultants – Chief Resident Engineer, Senior/Deputy Experts, Project/Section/Design/QS & Billing/Planning Engineers, etc.
basic_details.short_information: The latest RITES Ltd. (RITES) Individual Consultant (Young Professional) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for multiple specialised posts (VC Nos. YP/16/26 to YP/38/26). Published on 06 May 2026 at the official website https://www.rites.com, this recruitment drive offers engagements for highly experienced consultants in Civil, Electrical, S&T, Geotechnical, Design, QS & Billing, Planning, and related railway infrastructure roles. Eligible candidates aged up to 63 years with the required educational qualification and post-qualification experience can apply online from 06 May 2026 to 26 May 20

important_dates.interview_date: To be notified later on RITES website

age_limit.age_details: Contractual Individual Consultants (Independent, not regular staff)
age_limit.maximum_age: Not exceeding 63 years for all categories (no relaxation mentioned).
age_limit.details: For all Individual Consultant posts, the maximum age limit is 63 years (as on last date of application), with overall engagement not extendable beyond 65 years.

qualification.details: Nationality: Candidates must satisfy eligibility and experience requirements as per RITES norms; retired government / railway / PSU officers can also be engaged through competitive process for specific tasks and durations. | Educational Qualification & Experience (Illustrative by Major Categories)

salary_details.sr. contract expert / sr. qa/qc expert (: ₹70,000–₹80,000 per month (₹70,000 for Sr. Contract, ₹80,000 for Sr. QA/QC)
salary_details.details: Individual Consultants will be paid only for services rendered; they are not entitled to PF, ESI, gratuity or other statutory benefits, but applicable GST will be admissible and TDS will be deducted as per rules.

selection_process: 1. Online Application & Document Upload Candidates apply online via RITES website and upload all required documents (education, experience, ID proofs, etc.) in the portal; hard copies are not to be sent by post.

2. Document Scrutiny / Verification All applicants who submit the online form are provisionally allowed for document scrutiny; eligibility against the post is verified from uploaded documents and original proofs (in person/online).
3. Shortlisting Only candidates found eligible after document scrutiny are shortlisted for interview; RITES reserves the right to limit the number of candidates.
4. Interview (Personal) Mode of selection is Interview only; candidates are evaluated on technical skills, subject knowledge, experience, communication and interpersonal skills.

5. Client CV Approval & Engagement CVs of suitable candidates are sent to client; engagement letters issued only for candidates whose CVs are approved by client and subject to project/award requirements.
how_to_apply: Visit the RITES official website https://www.rites.com and open the “Career” section, then the relevant online registration link for Individual Consultant posts (VC Nos. YP/16/26–YP/38/26).
Carefully read the detailed advertisement and ensure that you meet the required educational qualification, post-qualification experience and age criteria for the specific VC number/post.

Fill the online application (registration format) with accurate personal details, educational qualifications, experience, category and identity proof details; note and preserve the system-generated Registration No. for all future communication.

Upload scanned copies of required documents: photograph, High School certificate (DOB), all marksheets and degree/diploma certificates (X, XII, Diploma, Graduation, Post-Graduation, PhD), experience certificates, relieving/NOC for Govt./PSU employees, ID proofs (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport/Voter ID/Driving Licence), and any additional supporting documents, in clear, legible format.
Print and sign the submitted online Application Form containing Registration No.; keep it ready along with self-attested photocopies for document verification/interview.
At the time of document verification/interview (in-person or online), present all original documents along with one self-attested set and duly filled Annexure-A (document checklist).
Note that applications once submitted cannot be altered; candidates must ensure correctness and completeness before final submission.

Experienced professionals who satisfy the specified educational and experience criteria for the desired VC number can submit the online application form through the RITES career portal within the prescribed dates.

The application will be considered only if all required details are filled correctly, supporting documents are uploaded clearly, and the candidates successfully complete document scrutiny and interview stages as per RITES requirements.
Application Mode: Online only through RITES Career page – https://www.rites.com (no offline/postal applications accepted).
important_instructions: Candidates must carefully check their eligibility for the specific VC number before applying and ensure that all claims made in the application are fully supported by valid documents.
Management reserves the right to cancel/modify/alter the selection/engagement process at any stage and to vary the number of vacancies without notice.

Any false/incomplete information or suppression of material facts will lead to rejection of candidature and termination of engagement if discovered later. The period of training/internship is not counted as post-qualification experience; date of acquiring qualification is determined as per earliest of exam date/result/marksheet/degree issuance as defined in the notification. Consultants are independent service providers, not regular staff; no TA/DA is admissible for joining or attending interview, and they are responsible for their own insurance. Individual Consultants must avoid conflict of interest, maintain confidentiality under Official Secrets Act, and adhere to conduct standards including provisions related to sexual harassment. RITES may terminate the contract at any time (normally with one month’s notice); consultants also must give one month’s notice to resign. All recruitment-related communication and online interview links will be sent to the registered email ID; candidates should regularly check their email and RITES website for updates and FAQs.

faq.question: Q1. What is RITES Individual Consultant (Young Professional) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: RITES Ltd. has released a notification for engagement of Individual Consultants (Young Professionals/Experts) under VC Nos. YP/16/26 to YP/38/26 for highly experienced roles in Civil, Electrical, S&T, Geotechnical, Design, QS & Billing, Planning and QA/QC for Railway Infrastructure projects in Haryana.

faq.question: Q2. How many posts are available in RITES Individual Consultant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are multiple individual consultant posts (generally one or a few positions per VC number) including Chief Resident Engineer, Senior/Deputy Experts, Senior Resident Engineers, Geologists, Geotechnical Experts, Design Engineers, Section Engineers, QS & Billing and Planning Engineers, totalling around 30+ positions across VC Nos. YP/16/26 to YP/38/26.

faq.question: Q3. What are the application dates for RITES Individual Consultant (YP/16/26–YP/38/26) 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process starts on 06 May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 26 May 2026 up to 23:59 hrs, with age and eligibility reckoned as on 26 May 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for RITES Individual Consultant posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold the relevant Diploma/Graduate/PG degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.Tech/M.Sc, etc.) in Civil, Electrical, Electronics, S&T, Geology/Geotechnical or related disciplines and possess post‑qualification experience ranging from 4–15+ years in railway/metro/highway infrastructure, depending on the specific VC number and role.

faq.question: Q5. Who can apply for RITES Individual Consultant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Both serving professionals and retired Government/Railway/PSU/Metro officers with the prescribed qualification and experience can be engaged as Individual Consultants through a competitive process for specific tasks and durations, subject to the age and experience criteria laid down for each post.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for RITES Individual Consultant (Young Professional) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For all Individual Consultant posts under VC Nos. YP/16/26 to YP/38/26, the maximum age is 63 years as on 26 May 2026, and even with extensions the overall engagement cannot go beyond 65 years of age.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for RITES Individual Consultant 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee (NIL) for any category; candidates can apply online for RITES Individual Consultant posts without paying any fee.

faq.question: Q8. What is the remuneration for RITES Individual Consultant posts?
faq.answer: Monthly consolidated remuneration varies by post, for example: about ₹1,25,000 for Chief Resident Engineer/Sr. Geotechnical Expert, ₹1,30,000 for Project Engineer (Cut & Cover), ₹80,000 for Dy. Chief Design Experts, ₹70,000 for Senior Resident Engineers, ₹60,000–₹70,000 for Sr. QS & Billing/Contract Experts and ₹40,000–₹45,000 for Section/Design/QS/Planning Engineers, plus admissible GST and TDS deduction as per rules.
